quoteLast: till late in the day one's head      is distracted by the shrill cries of itinerant traders (to these      are now added the cries of the vendors of cheap newspapers),      the passage of carriages and carts of all descriptions,      street-singers, organ-grinders endless, the screeching of parrots      and barking of dogs exposed for sale by a _grocer_ on the      opposite side of the way, together with the swarming of his and      his neighbour's dirty children--all was hushed; not a footfall,      'not (a line that is not often applicable here) a drum was
